scenarioName: Asthma Triggered by Allergic Reaction — Overlapping Physiology

scenarioStory.history: 30-year-old male with severe allergic asthma (prior ICU admission, never intubated). Visiting a friend's house and didn't realize they had cats until he was already inside. Symptoms started about 20 minutes after arrival with wheezing and hives that have progressed rapidly. Used his rescue inhaler 4 times with minimal improvement.
scenarioStory.discussion: Identify overlapping asthma and allergic reaction physiology. Apply MATC Anaphylaxis protocol when criteria are met. Understand epinephrine's dual role as bronchodilator and anti-allergic. Manage both components without treating only the 'asthma' piece. Recognize biphasic anaphylaxis risk
scenarioStory.course: Arrival — Asthma + Allergic Reaction Overlap: Patient has overlapping bronchospasm (asthma) and distributive/mucocutaneous features (allergic reaction). Meets criteria for anaphylaxis (respiratory compromise + skin involvement from a known allergen). IM epinephrine is first-line and doubles as a bronchodilator. Also treat the asthma component aggressively. → Default — Rapid Improvement with Epi and Bronchodilators: IM epinephrine and nebulized bronchodilators take effect quickly. Urticaria stabilizes, wheezing decreases, SpO2 improves. Transport with continued observation for biphasic reaction.

patientInformation.patientCondition: Acute severe wheezing and hives after cat exposure

scenarioEvents.name: Arrival — Asthma + Allergic Reaction Overlap
scenarioEvents.description: Patient has overlapping bronchospasm (asthma) and distributive/mucocutaneous features (allergic reaction). Meets criteria for anaphylaxis (respiratory compromise + skin involvement from a known allergen). IM epinephrine is first-line and doubles as a bronchodilator. Also treat the asthma component aggressively.
relatedChecklist: Administer Methylprednisolone 125 mg IV or Dexamethasone 10 mg IV

scenarioEvents.name: Default — Rapid Improvement with Epi and Bronchodilators
scenarioEvents.description: IM epinephrine and nebulized bronchodilators take effect quickly. Urticaria stabilizes, wheezing decreases, SpO2 improves. Transport with continued observation for biphasic reaction.

checklist.title: Recognize anaphylaxis criteria: respiratory compromise + skin findings + known allergen exposure
Move patient away from ongoing allergen
Administer Epinephrine 0.3 mg IM (1 mg/mL) as first-line — treats BOTH components
Administer Albuterol 5 mg + Ipratropium 0.5 mg nebulized with repetition
Administer Methylprednisolone 125 mg IV or Dexamethasone 10 mg IV
Continuous cardiac and waveform capnography monitoring
Educate patient on trigger avoidance and updated Epi-pen
NS fluid bolus if BP drops
